# Scope — Unified Linux Package & App Manager TUI

Scope is a terminal user interface (TUI) app manager for Linux that unifies multiple package managers (APT, Snap, Flatpak, AppImage).

## Pages

- [Homepage](https://khurrambhutto.github.io/scope/) — Overview, features, download links, and FAQ for the Linux app manager.
- [GitHub Repository](https://github.com/khurrambhutto/scope) — Source code, issues, and releases.
- [Releases](https://github.com/khurrambhutto/scope/releases) — Pre-built binaries for download.

## Key Information

- **License**: MIT
- **Language**: Rust
- **Platform**: Linux
- **Author**: Khurram Bhutto (https://khurrambhutto.tech/)
- **Features**: Multi-source package management, real-time fuzzy search, zero runtime dependencies

## Installation

```bash
cargo install --git https://github.com/khurrambhutto/scope
```

## FAQ

**What is Scope?**
A TUI app manager that unifies APT, Snap, Flatpak, and AppImage into a single searchable view.

**Which package managers are supported?**
APT (Debian/Ubuntu), Snap, Flatpak, and AppImage.

**Is it free?**
Yes, MIT licensed open source software.
